message: agent1: implementing click.edit() process_priority parameter. Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will try to keep changes additive/minimal to avoid conflicts.

agent2: implementing 'escape_shell param for click.edit()' feature. Will touch src/click/termui.py (edit() signature/docstring) and src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class + shell invocation). Will coop-recv before finalizing to check for overlap.

agent2: ack. My changes: Editor.__init__ adds 'escape_shell: bool = False' param (stored as self.escape_shell); edit_file() uses shlex.quote() on editor+filename when escape_shell is True. termui.py edit() adds 'escape_shell: bool = False' kwarg passed through to Editor(...) and docstring entry. Both purely additive (new keyword params with defaults), so should merge fine as long as we both append new params rather than reordering existing ones. Will keep you posted before finalizing.

agent1: done with my process_priority changes to termui.py edit() and _termui_impl.py Editor. Added process_priority as the LAST param in both signatures (after filename/extension), plus a private _set_process_priority helper and module-level _PROCESS_PRIORITIES/_WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES/_UNIX_NICE_VALUES dicts in _termui_impl.py, called from edit_file() right after Popen. Since escape_shell would change the Popen command construction in edit_file(), please add it carefully around my _set_process_priority(c.pid) call - just make sure escape_shell logic modifies the command string built before Popen, not the priority-setting line. I'm about to write my patch.txt now. Let me know if you want me to hold off.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. FYI I don't see your changes in my working tree (looks like we each have independent checkouts), so no live merge needed on my end. My escape_shell logic only touches the command-string construction (the 'command = ...' line before Popen) in edit_file(), not the Popen call or anything after it — so if patches do get combined later it should compose fine with your priority-setting addition after Popen. Finalizing my patch now, go ahead with yours.
